name: gitea
description: Gitea operations via tea CLI. Use when user mentions: gitea, tea, or when git remote shows a Gitea instance.
capabilities: Detect Gitea repositories from git remote configuration
Guide user authentication with tea login commands
Create, list, view, and merge pull requests
Create, list, view, close, and comment on issues
Clone and fork repositories via tea CLI
author: ETeissonniere
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance for using the tea CLI tool. No executable code, no network calls, no file system access. Static findings are false positives from scanner misclassifying metadata fields, hash values, and markdown code blocks as security issues.
valueStatement: This skill provides guidance for interacting with Gitea instances using the tea command-line tool. It includes command references for pull requests, issues, and repository operations.
userTitle: Use tea CLI for Gitea repositories
limitations: Requires tea CLI to be installed on the system
Requires valid Gitea authentication before operations
Does not provide tea installation instructions
Does not modify Gitea server configuration

useCases.title: Manage CI pipelines
useCases.description: Check pipeline status and trigger runs on Gitea CI systems

useCases.title: Handle pull requests
useCases.description: Create, review, checkout, and merge PRs from the terminal

useCases.title: Track issues
useCases.description: Create and manage issue tracking directly from command line

promptTemplates.title: List open pull requests
promptTemplates.prompt: List all open pull requests in this Gitea repository using tea pr list

promptTemplates.title: Create new issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new issue with title and description using tea issue create

promptTemplates.title: Check pipeline status
promptTemplates.prompt: Check the CI pipeline status before merging this pull request

promptTemplates.title: Fork repository
promptTemplates.prompt: Fork this repository using tea repo fork command

bestPractices: Verify authentication with tea login list before performing operations
Use git status to check for uncommitted changes before creating PRs
Use -o json or -o yaml for machine-readable output in scripts
antiPatterns: Running tea commands without verifying authentication first
Creating PRs without checking for uncommitted local changes
Triggering new CI runs without checking current pipeline status

faq.question: What is tea?
faq.answer: Tea is the official command-line client for Gitea. It allows you to interact with Gitea repositories from the terminal.

faq.question: How do I install tea?
faq.answer: Download tea from the official Gitea website or install via package managers like Homebrew, Scoop, or Go.

faq.question: Does this skill work with GitHub or GitLab?
faq.answer: No. This skill specifically detects Gitea instances. For GitHub, use the official GitHub skill instead.

faq.question: Is my data safe with tea?
faq.answer: Tea stores authentication tokens locally. Always verify you are connecting to trusted Gitea instances before logging in.

faq.question: Can I use tea for scripting?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the -o json or -o yaml flags to get machine-readable output suitable for scripts and automation.

faq.question: What if tea commands fail?
faq.answer: Check authentication with tea login list, verify the remote URL points to a Gitea instance, and ensure tea is installed correctly.
